title: orchidotypus hispidulus
side_effects: Orchidotypus hispidulus can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory issues in some individuals. Ingesting the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause inflammation and blistering on the skin, while inhaling its pollen can trigger asthma-like symptoms. It is essential to handle the plant with caution and consult a medical professional before using it for any purpose.
